title: I Forgot
lyrics:
[verse 1]
Crunch! The granola bar’s all gone.
I left my lunch right by the spoon.
The milk’s still cold, the bus zooms near—
*splat* goes my heart! It’s sitting here!

[chorus]
I Forgot! I Forgot!
I swear I didn’t mean to do it!
I Forgot! I Forgot!
Now my stomach’s gonna sue it!

[verse 2]
The cheese is lonely in the fridge.
The apple rolls like a sad bridge.
My sandwich dreams of riding wheels—
*crash*! Mom’s keys say, “No big deals!”

[chorus]
I Forgot! I Forgot!
I swear I didn’t mean to do it!
I Forgot! I Forgot!
Now my stomach’s gonna sue it!

[ending]
I Forgot… I forgot…
Maybe I’ll eat the clock.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, ukulele and groovy bass, 110 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The kitchen clock reads 8:17 AM, the last granola bar wrapper crinkles in your hand, and Mom turns from the sink, keys jingling, to say, "Lunchbox?"—your stomach drops as you realize the blue plastic box sits on the counter, untouched. "I forgot," you blurt, already sprinting toward the fridge.
cover_description: Wild-eyed kid in pajamas sprints past the waving lunchbox, one sock on, granola bar held high like a torch.
